prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Ed Miliband, the leader of the Labour Party, was photographed eating a bacon sandwich for the London Evening Standard in May 2014. The image gained widespread attention and was used as a source of commentary and an internet meme in 2014 and 2015. It sparked a debate on how politicians should control their public image, with some criticizing the focus on the image and its resulting memes, while others were critical of Nazi propaganda.
B. A photograph of then-Labour Party leader Ed Miliband eating a bacon sandwich became a source of sustained commentary and the subject of an internet meme in 2014 and 2015. Taken for the London Evening Standard newspaper while Miliband was campaigning for local elections in May 2014, the image became well known following the popular perception of it as making Miliband appear awkward, error-prone and incapable of performing simple tasks. The image sparked several internet memes, both supportive and mocking, and fuelled a debate about the lengths to which politicians attempt to control their public image, as well as drawing criticism that the focus placed on the image and its resulting memes was motivated by antisemitism. Miliband dismissed the debate as unimportant. Others, including opponents, described the media focus
C. An image capturing Ed Miliband, who was at the time the leader of the Labour Party, consuming a bacon sandwich, generated extensive discussion and became an internet meme throughout 2014 and 2015. This photograph, captured for the London Evening Standard newspaper during Miliband's local election campaign in May 2014, gained significant notoriety because many perceived it as portraying Miliband as clumsy, prone to mistakes, and unable to execute basic actions. The picture subsequently ignited numerous internet memes, some expressing support and others ridicule. Furthermore, it ignited a discussion concerning the extent to which political figures endeavor to manage their public persona. It also drew accusations that the intense scrutiny directed at the image and the subsequent memes was driven by antisemitism. Miliband, however, dismissed the entire discussion as insignificant. Conversely, several individuals, including political adversaries, characterized the media's concentrated attention on the image...

Answer:

options.A: Ed Miliband, the leader of the Labour Party, was photographed eating a bacon sandwich for the London Evening Standard in May 2014. The image gained widespread attention and was used as a source of commentary and an internet meme in 2014 and 2015. It sparked a debate on how politicians should control their public image, with some criticizing the focus on the image and its resulting memes, while others were critical of Nazi propaganda.
options.B: A photograph of then-Labour Party leader Ed Miliband eating a bacon sandwich became a source of sustained commentary and the subject of an internet meme in 2014 and 2015. Taken for the London Evening Standard newspaper while Miliband was campaigning for local elections in May 2014, the image became well known following the popular perception of it as making Miliband appear awkward, error-prone and incapable of performing simple tasks. The image sparked several internet memes, both supportive and mocking, and fuelled a debate about the lengths to which politicians attempt to control their public image, as well as drawing criticism that the focus placed on the image and its resulting memes was motivated by antisemitism. Miliband dismissed the debate as unimportant. Others, including opponents, described the media focus
options.C: An image capturing Ed Miliband, who was at the time the leader of the Labour Party, consuming a bacon sandwich, generated extensive discussion and became an internet meme throughout 2014 and 2015.
